metadata.title: David Kinuthia
metadata.description: Senior product engineer — full-stack web, AI-ready features, and shipping with product sense

home.heroTitle: Senior product engineer - closing the gap between a strong idea and software that ships.
home.heroBody: I build full-stack web products, AI-powered workflows, and the infrastructure behind them for teams and founders who want one owner from architecture to the last pixel. I'm based in Nairobi and work with people around the world.
home.heroAvailability: I'm open to senior full-time roles, contracts from three months, and advisory work with early-stage startups.
home.assessmentLink: Not sure where to start? Take the fit assessment

about.title: About
about.identity: I'm a full-stack engineer who thinks in products. After years shipping across frontend, backend, integrations, and delivery, I've learned that the best work starts with sharper questions—not a longer backlog.
about.edge: I thrive where technical depth meets product intuition: scoping ambiguous work, building MVPs you can learn from, and hardening systems when they start to matter. Founders and leads tend to bring me in when they need someone who can own a problem end-to-end without constant hand-holding.
about.personal: Outside of code, I recharge by running, reading about how teams build, and chasing good coffee with friends.

invoicething.name: Invoicething
invoicething.problem: Service businesses need invoicing and payments that feel calm, not like a second job.
invoicething.solution: I designed and shipped a full-stack product with subscriptions, hosted documents, email flows, and payment rails wired for real money movement.
invoicething.result: It's live today as a focused tool operators can adopt without a training manual.
invoicething.techLine: Next.js · NestJS · Supabase · Stripe · Polar · Vercel · Docker · Sentry

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: If you're building something ambitious and need an engineer who can carry it from decision to delivery, I'd love to hear from you. Pick whatever channel is easiest.

services.title: Engineering partnerships for teams shipping AI where mistakes are expensive.
services.subtitle: Six ways I plug into a team — from a tight architectural rescue to a full 0-to-1 build — with one engineer accountable from the first whiteboard to the last deploy.
services.tiersSubtitle: Each service runs at the scale that matches your problem. We narrow it together on the discovery call.

micro.outcome: Architecture review, working spike, audit, or a written technical strategy you can act on.
micro.idealFor: Validating an approach before committing, second opinions, or unblocking one specific bottleneck.

mvp.scope: 1–3 months. One coherent product surface, end-to-end.
mvp.outcome: A working feature or product slice live in production, instrumented, and ready for your team to iterate on.
mvp.idealFor: Launching a new AI feature, replatforming a workflow, or building a focused 0→1 product.

enterprise.scope: 3+ months. Multiple workstreams, often with retained advisory alongside.
enterprise.outcome: A platform your team owns and operates — hardening, observability, runbooks, and handover baked in from day one.
enterprise.idealFor: Regulated rollouts, multi-team integrations, or work where mistakes are genuinely expensive.

aiIntegration.name: AI integration & architecture
aiIntegration.persona: For FinTech & B2B teams with critical legacy systems
aiIntegration.summary: Plug AI capabilities into existing production systems without putting transactions, uptime, or compliance at risk. I design parallel infrastructure that talks to your core engine instead of replacing it.
aiIntegration.included: Architecture review, threat model, isolated AI service design, eval harness for failure modes, rollout plan with rollback gates.
aiIntegration.upsells: Normally goes with: MLOps & infrastructure (Enterprise) for observability and cost controls.

secureAi.name: Secure & compliant AI deployment
secureAi.persona: For LegalTech, compliance, and regulated industries
secureAi.summary: On-prem or private-cloud AI systems for teams who can't let data phone home. Document indexing, retrieval, and summarization with full citation accuracy and an audit trail.
secureAi.included: Private RAG architecture, vector store sizing, citation/grounding evals, SOC2-friendly logging, deployment guide for your infra team.
secureAi.upsells: Normally goes with: Fractional CTO retainer for ongoing security review and pen-test triage.

aiNative.name: AI-native product engineering
aiNative.persona: For founders building 0→1 or evolving a legacy SaaS
aiNative.summary: Ship the magical UX you've been pitching the board. I build agentic workflows and AI features into the product surface — not as a chatbot bolted on the side, but as the way your product actually works.
aiNative.included: Product engineering end-to-end: data model, agent design, UI flows, instrumentation, and the unglamorous error states that make AI features feel reliable.
aiNative.upsells: Normally goes with: Agentic workflow automation when the win is on the back office, not the front-end.

agenticOps.name: Agentic workflow automation
agenticOps.persona: For ops-heavy teams in PropTech, services, and back-office
agenticOps.summary: Replace the army of analysts reading PDFs, chasing renewals, and reconciling spreadsheets. Agents that extract, flag, and draft — layered over the tools you already use, not another dashboard to log into.
agenticOps.included: Process audit, agent orchestration, integration with existing CRM/ERP, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and ROI measurement.
agenticOps.upsells: Normally goes with: Secure & compliant AI deployment when the documents can't leave your tenancy.

mlopsRescue.name: MLOps & infrastructure rescue
mlopsRescue.persona: For Series A/B teams whose AI features are on fire
mlopsRescue.summary: Cloud bill out of control? Inference latency unacceptable? CI/CD for models doesn't exist? I come in, stop the bleeding, and leave you with a platform your team can actually operate.
mlopsRescue.included: Inference cost audit, model serving setup (vLLM/equivalent), observability for LLMs, deployment pipelines, and a written runbook for the team taking it over.
mlopsRescue.upsells: Normally goes with: AI integration & architecture if the underlying design is what's making it expensive.

fractionalCto.name: Fractional CTO & technical advisory
fractionalCto.persona: For founders without a deeply technical co-founder
fractionalCto.summary: A senior engineer in your corner for hiring, architecture decisions, vendor selection, and the board conversations where buzzwords get expensive. Retainer-based, with a fixed monthly cadence.
fractionalCto.included: Weekly working sessions, ad-hoc decision support, technical review of contractor work, hiring loops, and a written technical strategy document you can hand to investors.
fractionalCto.upsells: Normally combined with one of the build engagements above when there's a specific delivery on the line.

for.funded: You're past pre-seed — there's revenue, funding, or an enterprise contract that makes engineering quality a board-level concern.
for.regulated: You operate in regulated, high-trust, or high-stakes environments where 'move fast and break things' isn't an option.
for.legacy: You have systems that work and matter, and you need AI integrated alongside them — not a green-field rebuild.
for.scale: You've been burned by a generic AI wrapper before and now you need someone who can show you the architecture, not just the demo.

fit.notForTitle: This probably isn't a fit if

notFor.ideaGuy: You're looking for someone to build your idea for equity, or you expect a $50K MVP for $3K.
notFor.agencyMiddleman: You're a non-technical agency owner who wants to white-label senior engineering work at a junior margin.
notFor.noTechBudget: You want AI because you read about it, but your business doesn't yet have the operational maturity (or budget) to maintain software you didn't build yourself.

discovery.title: 1. Discovery
discovery.body: A free 30-minute call to understand what you're trying to ship, what's in your way, and whether I'm the right person for it. No pitch — if it's not a fit, I'll say so and point you somewhere better.

scope.title: 2. Scope & shape
scope.body: If there's a fit, I scope the work in writing: the problem, the approach, the deliverables, and the price. You see the architecture before you see an invoice.

build.title: 3. Build & ship
build.body: I work as one of your engineers — in your repos, your standups, your decision threads — until the thing is live, instrumented, and your team owns it.

cta.title: Ready to scope your engagement?
cta.body: Two ways to start. Pick whichever matches where you are.
cta.principlesLink: How I think about the work

method.title: How to work with me
method.howToWorkParagraph1: I'm available for senior full-time roles, contract engagements from three months, and advisory work with early-stage startups.
method.howToWorkParagraph2: I work best with teams building ambitious products who need someone to own problems end-to-end—from architecture and tradeoffs to the interface details users feel every day.
method.howToWorkParagraph3: If that sounds like your team, visit the contact page or schedule a short intro—I'd love to see if there's a fit.
method.outro: If this resonates with you, I'd love to hear what you're building. Reach out and I'll gladly discuss whether I'm the right engineer to help.

userCentric.title: user-centric
userCentric.subTitle: Design for the user, build for the impact.
userCentric.description: I believe that truly impactful products solve real-world issues in ways that feel intuitive and natural. By embedding myself in the user's journey, I ensure that my technical solutions translate into meaningful experiences. I seek to work with teams that prioritize empathy and care deeply about the people using their product.

quality.title: Quality
quality.subTitle: Yes, ship fast - but don't ship junk.
quality.description: While moving fast is important, I believe quality should never be sacrificed for speed. I aim for a level of craftsmanship that results in reliable, scalable, and elegant solutions. My goal is to create products that stand the test of time, not just meet deadlines. I want to collaborate with teams that share a passion for getting the details right.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.subTitle: The best products come from diverse minds working together.
collaboration.description: I thrive in cross-functional environments where engineers, designers, and product teams come together to build something greater than the sum of its parts. I am seeking opportunities where collaborative problem-solving and shared-ownership are encouraged and prioritized.

iteration.title: Iteration
iteration.subTitle: Perfection is found in iteration, not in a single release.
iteration.description: Great products are born through cycles of learning, iterating, and refining. I'm a strong advocate for building MVPs that allow for quick feedback and continuous improvement. I look for partners who value feedback, understand the importance of iteration, and embrace continuous learning.

simplicity.title: Simplicity
simplicity.subTitle: Simple is powerful.
simplicity.description: In both software architecture and user experience, simplicity is key. I believe that the most elegant solutions are often the simplest, and that complexity should only be introduced where it provides genuine value. I'm looking for teams that appreciate the value of simplicity and strive for clarity in both code and design.

humanCenteredAi.title: Human-Centered AI
humanCenteredAi.subTitle: Design intelligent systems that enhance the human experience.
humanCenteredAi.description: I believe that the role of AI should be to optimize the world for humans. I'm inspired by ethical leaders in technology like Tristan Harris and Aza Raskin. I'm looking for teams that prioritize human-centered design and development, and that are committed to using AI to enhance the user experience.

notFound.title: Page not found!
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist. Please consider navigating back to the home page or any of the other pages on this website. Thanks.

error.title: Something went wrong!
error.body: An error occurred while loading this page. Please try again later.

assessment.metaDescription: A short assessment to see whether we are a fit — and which engagement matches your context.
assessment.title: Fit assessment
assessment.subtitle: A few sharp questions. Your answers shape a clear read on fit, the right service lane, and what to do next.
assessment.introLead: This takes most people under three minutes. You can pause anytime — we will email you a link to pick up where you left off.
assessment.consentLabel: I agree to be contacted about my results and understand how my data is used (see privacy note below).
assessment.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
assessment.privacyBody: We store your email and answers to score fit and follow up. Data is retained for up to 24 months unless you ask us to delete it sooner. You may request deletion anytime via the email on the contact page.
assessment.nurtureHint: When you are ready for a deeper conversation, book a discovery call — there is no pressure on timing.
assessment.resultsNotFit: Based on this pass, we are probably not the right engagement right now. You will still find useful material on the services page.

strong.label: Strong fit
strong.body: You look like a strong match for a focused strategy conversation first.

good.label: Good fit
good.body: You are a good match for a discovery call so we can qualify scope and timing together.

nurture.label: Early stage
nurture.body: You are early in the journey — start with the guide, then book when you are ready.

notFit.label: Not a fit (today)
notFit.body: That is okay — better to know now than after a long sales cycle.

challenge.title: What is the primary challenge right now?

options.integrationLegacy: Integrate AI with legacy / high-stakes systems
options.secureRegulated: Deploy AI in a regulated or confidential environment
options.productAiNative: Ship AI-native product UX (not a bolt-on chatbot)
options.opsAutomation: Automate ops-heavy workflows (documents, renewals, back office)

timeline.title: Desired timeline to first production milestone

priorBurn.title: Have you been burned by an AI vendor or internal spike before?

decisionAuthority.title: Authority to move an engagement forward

assessment.triagePlaybook: Triage: strong → strategy call within one business day. good → discovery within two days. nurture → send guide + one polite follow-up after 7 days. not_fit → no outbound sales; optional pointer to content only.
